title: Express Routing (Module 2) Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of the Express Router for modular route grouping and management in large-scale applications.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily role of 'Routing' in an Express.js application?
options: Defining which code should run when a user visits a specific URL or performs a specific action (like a POST request).
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which Express feature allows you to create 'modular' route handlers in separate files for better organization?
Which and/or what was the primarily benefit of using separate route files (e.g., productRoutes.js)?
options: Better code organization and easier maintenance as the project grows.

questions.question: Which HTTP method should be used in a professional REST API specifically to 'delete' a resource?
Does Express allowing the use of multiple handlers for a single route using the 'next()' function?
options: Yes, as it is a core feature of the Express middleware system.
No, only one handler per route is allowed.
